skill.name: Linux Production Shell Scripts
skill.description: Production-ready bash script templates for Linux system administration including backups, monitoring, user management, log analysis, and automation tasks.
skill.summary: Ready-to-use shell scripts for Linux admins to automate backups, monitor systems, manage users, analyze logs, and schedule tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 170 patterns but evaluation confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains legitimate bash scripting templates for system administration. Command substitution, sudo usage, and /proc access are expected patterns for educational shell scripts. No malicious intent detected. Minor warnings apply due to sudo privilege escalation patterns which should be documented for users.

low_findings.title: Sudo Privilege Escalation
low_findings.description: Scripts use sudo for package installation and service management. Legitimate for system administration but requires user awareness of privilege requirements.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicit sudo commands for apt-get and systemctl - standard system administration patterns requiring elevated privileges.

low_findings.title: System File Access
low_findings.description: Scripts read /etc/passwd for user management and /proc/loadavg for system monitoring. These are world-readable system files used legitimately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Linux system files accessed for legitimate administrative purposes - user enumeration and load monitoring.

low_findings.title: Cron Job Modification
low_findings.description: Script demonstrates crontab modification for task scheduling. Legitimate automation functionality but users should understand cron security implications.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Crontab commands used for legitimate task scheduling - expected functionality for automation scripts.

content.user_title: Create Production Shell Scripts for Linux Automation
content.value_statement: System administrators need reliable, tested scripts for routine tasks without writing from scratch. This skill provides production-ready bash templates for backups, monitoring, user management, and automation with best practices built in.
actual_capabilities: Generate backup scripts for files, directories, databases, and remote servers with rotation policies
Create system monitoring scripts for CPU, disk, memory usage with alerting thresholds
Build user management scripts for account creation, password expiry checks, and bulk operations
Write log analysis scripts for error extraction, web server log parsing, and trend analysis
Develop network diagnostic scripts for connectivity testing, uptime monitoring, and interface reporting
Generate automation scripts for package installation, service management, and scheduled tasks
limitations: Scripts are templates requiring customization of paths, credentials, and thresholds for your environment
Sudo-privileged scripts require appropriate user permissions and should be tested in non-production first
Database backup scripts support MySQL/MariaDB - other databases need adapter modifications
Remote operations via SSH require pre-configured key-based authentication for automation

use_cases.title: Automated Backup System
use_cases.description: Set up automated daily backups of critical directories with rsync to remote servers, automatic rotation to keep only recent backups, and database dumps for complete disaster recovery.

use_cases.title: Server Health Monitoring
use_cases.description: Deploy monitoring scripts to track CPU, disk, and memory usage with configurable alert thresholds. Generate daily health reports and receive notifications when resources exceed limits.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers managing multiple Linux servers

use_cases.title: User Account Lifecycle Management
use_cases.description: Automate user provisioning with account creation scripts, generate password expiry reports for compliance audits, and bulk-manage accounts across the organization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Backup Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bash script to backup the /var/www directory to /backup daily, keeping only the last 7 backups. Include timestamped filenames and error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple directory backup with rotation

prompt_templates.title: System Monitoring Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a monitoring script that checks CPU usage, disk space, and memory. Send an alert email to admin@example.com when any metric exceeds 85%. Log all readings to a file with timestamps.

prompt_templates.title: Database Backup Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a script to backup all MySQL databases, compress each dump file, upload to an S3 bucket, and delete local copies older than 14 days. Include error handling and logging.

prompt_templates.title: Server Fleet Health Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a script that connects to multiple servers via SSH, collects system information (hostname, OS, CPU, memory, disk), and generates a consolidated HTML report. Include uptime and top processes for each server.

output_examples.input: Create a script to monitor disk usage and alert when it exceeds 90%
output_examples.output: A complete bash script with disk usage monitoring, threshold checking, alert notification via email, and logging functionality. The script includes proper error handling, variable documentation, and can be scheduled via cron for automated execution.

output_examples.input: Generate a backup script for MySQL database with rotation
output_examples.output: Production-ready script that performs mysqldump with credentials handling, gzip compression, timestamped filenames, automatic deletion of backups older than 7 days, and detailed logging. Includes usage instructions and configuration section.

best_practices: Always test scripts in non-production environments before deploying to production servers
Use absolute paths in scripts to avoid failures when executed from different directories
Quote all variables to handle paths and values containing spaces or special characters
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode passwords or API keys in scripts - use environment variables or secure secret management
Avoid running scripts as root unless absolutely necessary - use sudo for specific commands instead
Never skip error checking - always verify command success with proper exit code handling

faq.question: How do I make these scripts executable?
faq.answer: Run chmod +x script_name.sh to make the script executable, then run it with ./script_name.sh. Alternatively, run bash script_name.sh without changing permissions.

faq.question: Do I need root privileges to run these scripts?
faq.answer: Some scripts require elevated privileges for operations like user management, package installation, or service control. Scripts using sudo will prompt for your password. Test each script to understand its permission requirements.

faq.question: Can I schedule these scripts to run automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, use cron to schedule scripts. Run crontab -e and add entries like '0 2 * * * /path/to/script.sh' to run daily at 2 AM. The skill includes examples of cron setup.

faq.question: How do I customize these scripts for my environment?
faq.answer: Each script has configuration variables at the top (paths, thresholds, credentials). Modify these values to match your environment. Always test in a safe environment before production use.

faq.question: What if a script fails or produces errors?
faq.answer: Run scripts with bash -x script.sh to enable debug mode and see each command as it executes. Check system logs at /var/log/syslog for service-related errors. Ensure all required utilities are installed.

faq.question: Are these scripts compatible with all Linux distributions?
faq.answer: Most scripts work on any bash-compatible Linux system. Package management commands (apt-get) are Debian/Ubuntu specific. For RHEL/CentOS, replace with yum or dnf equivalents. The skill notes distribution-specific commands where applicable.
